title: Modern Operations Research with Python
text:
0.1 Welcome
This ebook provides a practical, code-first introduction to modern Operations Research using Python.
Current Chapter - 2. Linear Programming with PuLP
More chapters coming soon: Foundations, Integer Programming, Predict-then-Optimize, etc.
crumbs: 1  Modern Operations Research with Python

title: 2  2. Linear Programming with PuLP
text:
2.1 Introduction
Linear Programming (LP) is a core technique in Operations Research used to optimize a linear objective subject to linear constraints.
In this chapter we solve the classic Diet Problem.
crumbs: 2  2. Linear Programming with PuLP

title: 2  2. Linear Programming with PuLP
text:
2.2 Mathematical Formulation
Decision variables (\(x_1\) = servings of Steak, \(x_2\) = Chicken, \(x_3\) = Rice, \(x_4\) = Broccoli)
\[
\begin{align*}
\text{Minimize } & 2.5x_1 + 1.8x_2 + 0.5x_3 + 0.6x_4 \\
\text{s.t.} \quad
& 5x_1 + 6x_2 + x_3 + 2x_4 \geq 50 \quad \text{(Protein)} \\
& 2x_1 + x_2 + 25x_3 + 5x_4 \geq 200 \quad \text{(Carbs)} \\
& 8x_1 + 4x_2 + 0.5x_3 + 0.3x_4 \leq 80 \quad \text{(Fat)} \\
& x_i \geq 0 \quad \forall i
\end{align*}
\]
crumbs: 2  2. Linear Programming with PuLP

title: 2  2. Linear Programming with PuLP
text:
2.3 PuLP Implementation
```python from pulp import *
prob = LpProblem(“Diet_Problem”, LpMinimize)
steak = LpVariable(“Steak”, lowBound=0) chicken = LpVariable(“Chicken”, lowBound=0) rice = LpVariable(“Rice”, lowBound=0) broccoli = LpVariable(“Broccoli”, lowBound=0)
prob += 2.5steak + 1.8chicken + 0.5rice + 0.6broccoli
prob += 5steak + 6chicken + rice + 2broccoli >= 50, “Protein” prob += 2steak + chicken + 25rice + 5broccoli >= 200, “Carbs” prob += 8steak + 4chicken + 0.5rice + 0.3broccoli <= 80, “Fat”
prob.solve(PULP_CBC_CMD(msg=0))
print(“Status:”, LpStatus[prob.status]) print(“Total Cost: $”, round(value(prob.objective), 2))
for v in prob.variables(): print(v.name, “=”, round(value(v), 2))
crumbs: 2  2. Linear Programming with PuLP
